output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Send a 60-Second Demand Letter
output.meta_description: Your landlord can't just keep your security deposit. Learn your tenant rights and send a state-specific demand letter in 60 seconds for only $19.99.
content_outline: The Illegal Hold: What Landlords Actually Can't Deduct From Your Deposit
State Deadline Cheat Sheet: How Long Is Too Long to Wait
Skip the Lawyer: The 60-Second Demand Letter That Forces Landlords to Pay
Deposits-Back.com vs. Small Claims Court: Save Time and Money
What to Do If Your Landlord Still Refuses to Return Your Deposit
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days and forfeit deductions if they miss the deadline
Normal wear and tear, pre-existing damage, and fees not listed in the lease are generally illegal deductions
A formal demand letter citing state statutes is often enough to force immediate payment without filing in court
Deposits-Back.com lets tenants generate and send a state-specific security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for a one-time $19.99 fee with no signup or subscription required
output.cta: Stop waiting and start demanding. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds for just $19.99. No signup. No subscription. Just your money back.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, delaying, or deducting from their security deposit, especially those who feel trapped by the cost and time of legal action.

short_form_scripts.hook: Can your landlord keep your deposit for carpet cleaning?
short_form_scripts.body: Nope. Normal wear and tear is NOT deductible in most states. If your landlord missed the deadline or invented fake fees, you can demand the full amount back with a formal letter.
short_form_scripts.cta: Send your legal demand letter in 60 seconds at Deposits-Back.com.
